tabledata.control_description: Foundation: Principles for using the GRI Standards
General Disclosures: Strategy, Ethics, Governance, Stakeholders
Management Approach: Materiality and Impact Management
Freedom of Association and Collective Bargaining

tabledata.notes: Ensure processing is lawful, fair, and transparent to data subjects.
Data collected must be for specified, explicit and legitimate purposes.
Collect only data necessary for the stated purpose.
Maintain and update accurate personal data.
Retain data only as long as necessary for processing purposes.
Secure personal data against unauthorized or unlawful processing.
Demonstrate compliance with GDPR principles at all times.
Individuals must receive clear and accessible info on data usage.
Must respond to subject access requests within 30 days.
Data subjects can correct inaccurate or incomplete data.

tabledata.control_description: Right to Erasure ("Right to be Forgotten")
tabledata.notes: Data must be erased if no legal ground for retention exists.
Individuals can block or limit processing of their data.
Must provide data in a structured, portable format.
Individuals can object to processing for direct marketing or profiling.
Individuals can demand human review of algorithmic decisions.
Apply measures like encryption, pseudonymization, access control.
Conduct DPIAs for high-risk processing (e.g. biometrics, profiling).
Maintain internal records of all personal data handling activities.
Embed privacy controls at the architecture and system level.
Capture, manage, and track opt-ins with verifiable consent records.
Ensure all vendors handling personal data have GDPR-aligned contracts.
Establish contracts when two or more parties jointly control data.
Use SCCs, adequacy decisions, or binding corporate rules.
Mandatory for large-scale monitoring or sensitive data processing.
Cooperate with and respond to data protection authorities promptly.
Conduct periodic reviews of data protection controls and gaps.
All staff must be aware of data protection roles and responsibilities.
Report breaches within 72 hours to the authority; notify individuals if needed.
Privacy policy must be updated, clear, and visible to users.
Clearly define how long data is retained and automate deletion when expired.
Channel to report internal violations or breaches confidentially.
Conduct LIA when using "legitimate interest" as a basis for data use.
Collect granular cookie consents, offer opt-out, and update banners as needed.
Tag personal data and restrict access using RBAC or attribute-based controls.
Encrypt and anonymize sensitive datasets wherever applicable.

Version control, change tracking for EUCs
Board level review of financial controls

Validate accuracy of financial reports
